contents:
# USNS Robert E. Peary Completes 154-Day Deployment Supporting 4th Fleet Combat Readiness

## Overview
The U.S. Navy's USNS Robert E. Peary returned to Naval Station Norfolk after a 154-day deployment, having supported Operation Southern Spear by supplying 17 Navy vessels. This mission focused on counter-narcotics efforts to dismantle designated terrorist organizations and counter illicit maritime activity in the Western Hemisphere.[1]

## Details
USNS Robert E. Peary (T-AKE 5), a Lewis and Clark-class dry cargo ship operated by Military Sealift Command Atlantic (MSCLANT), concluded its deployment on April 29, 2026, with its crew of 105 civil service mariners (CIVMARS).[1] The ship supported Operation Southern Spear (OSS), a counter-narcotics operation targeting threats to Western Hemisphere security, by resupplying vessels including the USS Gerald R. Ford.[1] The deployment enhanced U.S. 4th Fleet combat readiness in its area of responsibility.[1] The return stayed announced by LaShawn Sykes of USN Military Sealift Command on April 30, 2026.[1]

## Location
Naval Station Norfolk, Virginia; U.S. 4th Fleet area of responsibility

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @thewarzonewire (The War Zone Wire). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.
